refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall provide analysis of the worksite terrain.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR combined multiple obligations: analyzing terrain, obstacles, and work-zone representation. To ensure atomicity and solution neutrality, these are separated into distinct sub-FRs focusing solely on what the system must do without implying how.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify that the system can generate and present an analysis of the terrain independently of obstacle or work-zone data, through test cases or demonstrations.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall provide analysis of the worksite terrain.
The NGHE solution shall provide analysis of obstacles within the worksite area.
The NGHE solution shall provide representation of the work-zone within the worksite area.

candidate_dps: Generate a digital terrain model representing key site features
Extract and classify terrain features relevant to planning and safety
Create and update a geospatial map integrating terrain and operational constraints
Provide an interface to share the terrain model/map with planning and execution modules
selected_dp.selected_dp: Create and update a geospatial map integrating terrain and operational constraints

potential_sources: Integration of terrain data with operational policies may create dependencies if not modularized
coupling_check.notes: Selecting a geospatial map that integrates terrain and operational constraints supports a unified representation accessible by planning and execution, minimizing coupling if interfaces are well defined.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of accurate terrain data sources
Risk that operational policies may change frequently requiring map updates
Assumes planning and execution modules can consume the geospatial map format
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly separating terrain data acquisition and operational policy integration into distinct DPs to further reduce coupling and improve modularity.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the core system functionalities including data processing, map creation, and integration of terrain and operational constraints for geospatial awareness.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives sensor and vehicle status data relevant to terrain and operational conditions.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives operational commands and updates map-related tasks; sends back status updates.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Handles communication with external systems for map data exchange and updates.

module_containment_scores.notes: Highly cohesive as map creation and integration logically belong here; minimal diffusion as most data processing is internal.
Vehicle module primarily handles physical control and telemetry; map creation would cause significant diffusion outside this module.
Teleoperation focuses on remote control and command interfaces; integrating map creation would diffuse responsibilities and increase coupling.
Safety Services focus on alerts and safety monitoring; map creation is outside its core scope, causing high diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary for containing the DP 'Create and update a geospatial map integrating terrain and operational constraints' because it centralizes data processing and map integration functions, minimizing diffusion and maximizing cohesion.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on timely and accurate telemetry data from Vehicle module may cause interface complexity.
If map updates require frequent interaction with Teleoperation or Safety Services, interface management could become complex.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP 'Create and update a geospatial map integrating terrain and operational constraints' directly addresses the FR 'The NGHE solution shall provide analysis of the worksite terrain' by specifying the creation and updating of a geospatial map that integrates terrain and operational constraints, fulfilling the functional requirement of terrain analysis.

diffusion_risks: Dependency on timely and accurate telemetry data from the Vehicle module may introduce interface complexity and potential diffusion risk if data handling is not well encapsulated.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module has a high containment score (9) and low diffusion score (1), indicating strong cohesion and minimal diffusion. The DP is well contained within this module, which centralizes data processing and map integration functions, minimizing diffusion and maximizing cohesion.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible as the acceptance criteria specify that a worksite model/map must be generated and available to planning/execution functions, which can be tested through system integration and functional tests.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation is clear since the NGHE Solution module encompasses the core functionalities including data processing, map creation, and integration of terrain and operational constraints, directly linking the FR to the IM boundary.

recommended_revisions: Monitor and manage interface complexity arising from dependencies on telemetry data to avoid diffusion across modules.
Ensure clear interface definitions and data contracts between NGHE Solution and Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules to maintain low diffusion and high cohesion.
Consider periodic reviews of module interactions to detect and mitigate any emerging diffusion risks as system evolves.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with strong justification, clear allocation, and feasible verification. The DP is well contained within the NGHE Solution module with minimal diffusion risk, and the functional requirement is fully addressed by the current DP and IM. Given the high safety criticality, it is prudent to maintain a clear and stable decomposition at this level and focus on monitoring interface complexity and diffusion risks rather than further decomposing at this time.

revise.why: To monitor and manage interface complexity arising from dependencies on telemetry data and ensure clear interface definitions and data contracts between modules to maintain low diffusion and high cohesion.

assumptions: Telemetry data from the Vehicle module will be timely and accurate as required.
The NGHE Solution module will maintain high cohesion and low diffusion as system evolves.
open_risks: Potential interface complexity and diffusion risk due to dependency on telemetry data from Vehicle module.
Emerging diffusion risks as system evolves and modules interact.
evidence_needed: System integration and functional test results verifying the generation and availability of the worksite model/map.
Periodic review reports on module interactions and interface complexity.
